title: Getting Started with QuickExplore
text: QuickExplore provides point--click Shiny interface modelled SAS Studio’s library/dataset browser. supports SAS (.sas7bdat, .xpt), CSV, R (.rds) files lets explore, filter, summarise, export datasets without writing code. package also exposes set standalone R functions use scripts Shiny applications.

title: Getting Started with QuickExplore
text: app opens default browser. running inside RStudio opens Viewer pane.

title: Getting Started with QuickExplore
text: Click Add Library left sidebar. Give library short name (e.g. MYLIB) enter path folder contains data files. Click dataset name list load . Use Data, Summary, Convert tabs explore export.

title: Getting Started with QuickExplore
text: helper functions used internally app exported can called directly.

title: Getting Started with QuickExplore
code: df <- data.frame(   age  = c(25, 34, 45, 52, 28, NA),   sex  = c("M", "F", "M", "F", "M", "F"),   dose = c(10, 20, 10, 30, 20, 10) )  # Numeric summary library(QuickExplore) compute_numeric_summary(df, c("age", "dose")) #>   Variable N Missing  Mean Median    SD Min Max #> 1      age 5       1 36.80     34 11.43  25  52 #> 2     dose 6       0 16.67     15  8.16  10  30  # Categorical summary compute_categorical_summary(df, "sex") #>   sex Frequency Percentage Variable #> 1   F         3         50      sex #> 2   M         3         50      sex

title: Getting Started with QuickExplore
text: tab application implemented reusable Shiny module. can embed modules Shiny apps: Available module pairs:
code: library(shiny) library(QuickExplore)  ui <- fluidPage(   data_viewer_ui("viewer") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   my_data <- reactiveVal(mtcars)   my_path <- reactiveVal("mtcars")    # or a real file path   data_viewer_server("viewer", my_data, my_path) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: QuickExplore — Interactive Dataset Explorer
text: QuickExplore provides point--click Shiny interface modelled SAS Studio’s library/dataset browser. supports SAS (.sas7bdat, .xpt), CSV, R (.rds) files lets explore, filter, summarise, export datasets without writing code. package also exposes set standalone R functions use scripts Shiny applications.

title: QuickExplore — Interactive Dataset Explorer
text: r{eval = FALSE} library(QuickExplore) run_app() app opens default browser. running inside RStudio opens Viewer pane.

title: QuickExplore — Interactive Dataset Explorer
text: Click Add Library left sidebar. Give library short name (e.g. MYLIB) enter path folder contains data files. Click dataset name list load . Use Data, Summary, Convert tabs explore export.

title: QuickExplore — Interactive Dataset Explorer
text: helper functions used internally app exported can called directly.

title: QuickExplore — Interactive Dataset Explorer
code: df <- data.frame(   age  = c(25, 34, 45, 52, 28, NA),   sex  = c("M", "F", "M", "F", "M", "F"),   dose = c(10, 20, 10, 30, 20, 10) )  # Numeric summary library(QuickExplore) compute_numeric_summary(df, c("age", "dose"))  # Categorical summary compute_categorical_summary(df, "sex")

code: library(shiny) library(QuickExplore)  ui <- fluidPage(   data_viewer_ui("viewer") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   my_data <- reactiveVal(mtcars)   my_path <- reactiveVal("mtcars")    # or a real file path   data_viewer_server("viewer", my_data, my_path) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

what: QuickExplore: Interactive Dataset Explorer for SAS and Other Data Formats — QuickExplore-package
title: QuickExplore: Interactive Dataset Explorer for SAS and Other Data Formats — QuickExplore-package
text: 'Shiny' application provides SAS Studio-style interface browsing, exploring, summarising, converting datasets SAS (.sas7bdat, .xpt), CSV, R (.rds) formats. main entry point run_app().  package also exports several standalone helper functions useful outside Shiny context: read_dataset() – read supported format data frame. list_datasets() – enumerate datasets directory. get_variable_info() – variable-level metadata. compute_numeric_summary() / compute_categorical_summary() – tidy descriptive statistics. format_file_size() – human-readable file size strings.

title: QuickExplore: Interactive Dataset Explorer for SAS and Other Data Formats — QuickExplore-package
text: Maintainer: Ram Gaduputi ramsas88@gmail.com Authors: Jagadish Katam Jagadish.katam@gmail.com

what: Compute frequency statistics for categorical variables — compute_categorical_summary
title: Compute frequency statistics for categorical variables — compute_categorical_summary
text: Returns value frequencies percentages non-numeric variable vars, optionally grouped second variable.

title: Compute frequency statistics for categorical variables — compute_categorical_summary
Compute frequency statistics for categorical variables — compute_categorical_summary
text: df data.frame tibble. vars Character vector variable names summarise. group_var Optional character string naming grouping variable. Pass NULL (default) grouping.

title: Compute frequency statistics for categorical variables — compute_categorical_summary
text: data.frame columns grouping variable (), value, frequency count, percentage, variable name. Returns NULL categorical variables vars.

title: Compute frequency statistics for categorical variables — compute_categorical_summary
code: df <- data.frame(sex = c("M","F","M","F","M"), trt = c("A","A","B","B","A")) compute_categorical_summary(df, c("sex", "trt")) #>    sex Frequency Percentage Variable  trt #> 1    F         2         40      sex <NA> #> 2    M         3         60      sex <NA> #> 3 <NA>         3         60      trt    A #> 4 <NA>         2         40      trt    B

what: Compute summary statistics for numeric variables — compute_numeric_summary
title: Compute summary statistics for numeric variables — compute_numeric_summary
text: Returns tidy data frame N, mean, median, standard deviation, minimum, maximum numeric variable vars.

title: Compute summary statistics for numeric variables — compute_numeric_summary
Compute summary statistics for numeric variables — compute_numeric_summary
text: df data.frame tibble. vars Character vector variable names summarise. group_var Optional character string naming grouping variable. Pass NULL (default) grouping.

title: Compute summary statistics for numeric variables — compute_numeric_summary
text: data.frame (one row per variable, per variable × group level) NULL numeric variables vars.

title: Compute summary statistics for numeric variables — compute_numeric_summary
code: df <- data.frame(x = rnorm(100), y = runif(100), g = rep(c("A", "B"), 50)) compute_numeric_summary(df, c("x", "y")) #>   Variable   N Missing Mean Median   SD   Min  Max #> 1        x 100       0 0.07   0.09 1.05 -2.61 2.76 #> 2        y 100       0 0.52   0.55 0.31  0.02 0.98 compute_numeric_summary(df, c("x", "y"), group_var = "g") #> # A tibble: 4 × 9 #>   g     Variable     N Missing  Mean Median    SD   Min   Max #>   <chr> <chr>    <int>   <int> <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 A     x           50       0  0.02   0.15  1.12 -2.61  2.07 #> 2 B     x           50       0  0.12  -0.01  0.99 -1.91  2.76 #> 3 A     y           50       0  0.5    0.53  0.29  0.04  0.98 #> 4 B     y           50       0  0.54   0.61  0.32  0.02  0.98

text: Handles library registration, dataset listing, dataset loading sidebar browser panel.

text: id Character string. Shiny module namespace identifier. selected_dataset shiny::reactiveVal() stores full file path currently selected dataset. loaded_data shiny::reactiveVal() stores loaded data.frame.

title: QuickExplore 0.1.0
text: run_app() launches Dataset Explorer Shiny application. Browse multiple directory-based libraries simultaneously. Load datasets SAS (.sas7bdat, .xpt), CSV, R (.rds) formats. Interactive data viewer column-level filtering via DT. Explore panel dplyr-expression filtering quick-filter badges. Variable Explorer type-coloured metadata top-value frequency tables. Summary panel automatic numeric categorical statistics, plus missing-value heat bar table. Converter panel exports .rds, .xlsx, .csv, .json, SAS V5 transport (.xpt). Exported utility functions: read_dataset(), list_datasets(), get_variable_info(), get_dataset_metadata(), format_file_size(), compute_numeric_summary(), compute_categorical_summary().
